title: French Kitchen
editorialSummary: French cooking techniques grouped by the heat or medium applied; the false trail is that sauteing and stir-frying both use high heat with fat in a pan, but saute is a French technique while stir-fry belongs to Chinese cuisine — solvers must match each to its correct culinary tradition. Poeler is intentionally classified as dry-heat because the defining characteristic is the exposed dry-heat searing phase before covering.
